title: viola tenuicornis subsp. ircutiana
intro: Viola tenuicornis subsp. ircutiana, also known as the Ircut violet, is a rare, subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the viola genus. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The Ircut violet has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, such as digestive problems and respiratory infections, due to its health benefits of soothing and calming the body. Herbal preparations of the Ircut violet include infusions and decoctions, which can be consumed as teas or used topically as poultices for skin conditions.
